name: CSRF — SameSite=Lax Bypass
educational_objective: Understand that SameSite=Lax protects against cross-site POST requests, but still allows cookies on top-level GET navigations. If a state-changing action uses GET, SameSite=Lax provides zero protection.
scenario: AccountHub — a user account management portal. The admin configured SameSite=Lax on session cookies, thinking it prevents all CSRF. However, the /account/role-update endpoint accepts GET requests, making it vulnerable to top-level navigation CSRF.
ar_scenario: AccountHub تستخدم SameSite=Lax على الكوكي. لكن /account/role-update يقبل GET — مما يجعله ضعيفاً أمام CSRF عبر top-level navigation.

hints_metadata.content: SameSite=Lax blocks cross-site POST but allows GET on top-level navigations. Does any sensitive endpoint accept GET requests?
hints_metadata.ar_content: SameSite=Lax يمنع POST عبر cross-site لكن يسمح بـ GET في top-level navigations. هل هناك نقطة حساسة تقبل GET؟

hints_metadata.content: The /account/role-update?role=admin endpoint performs a state change via GET. A top-level navigation (like window.location redirect) carries the SameSite=Lax cookie.
hints_metadata.ar_content: نقطة /account/role-update?role=admin تُجري تغييراً عبر GET. التنقل على مستوى أعلى يحمل كوكي SameSite=Lax.

hints_metadata.content: Click 'Simulate Victim Navigation' — this triggers a top-level GET to /account/role-update?role=admin. The Lax cookie is sent, the role is updated. That's the bypass.
hints_metadata.ar_content: اضغط 'Simulate Victim Navigation' — يُطلق GET على المستوى الأعلى لـ /account/role-update. كوكي Lax يُرسل والـ role يتغيّر.

flag_validation.note: Returned from /simulate-victim-navigation when GET-based state change is triggered cross-site

hidden_solution_path.exploit_path: Attacker page → window.location = 'https://accounthub.corp/account/role-update?role=admin' → top-level nav → Lax cookie sent → role updated
hidden_solution_path.fix_summary: Use SameSite=Strict; never perform state changes on GET; add CSRF token on all mutating endpoints
